title: tristicha trifaria subsp. tlatlayana
intro: Tristicha trifaria subsp. tlatlayana, also known as tlatlaya, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower fever, reduce inflammation, and treat various respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, which have been reported to provide numerous health benefits, such as reducing coughs, relieving congestion, and soothing skin irritations. In traditional medicine, tlatlaya is often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to promote overall well-being and vitality.
